$schema_note: This is a human- and AI-readable master record for the Roll30 project. It is NOT source code and is not consumed by the app at runtime. It exists to give any person or AI instant context on what this project is, how it works, and its full change history.

disclaimer_and_usage_rules.what_this_file_is: A single source of truth describing the Roll30 project: its purpose, structure, how it works, and a running log of every change made to it.
how_the_two_sections_relate: 'project_state' is the DOCUMENT: a fuller description of how the app currently works.
'change_log' is the LIST: a running, append-only history where a new entry is added EVERY time any change is made.
disclaimer_and_usage_rules.priority_rule: THE LIST WINS. If anything in 'change_log' conflicts with anything in 'project_state', the 'change_log' is correct and 'project_state' is simply not yet fully updated. Always trust the most recent relevant 'change_log' entries over 'project_state'.
disclaimer_and_usage_rules.ALWAYS_PUSH_TO_GITHUB: MANDATORY, NON-NEGOTIABLE: every single time you make ANY change to this project (code, data, config, this master document — anything at all), you MUST commit and push it to GitHub before considering the task done. Never leave a change sitting only on the local machine. Always always always push.
instructions_for_any_ai: 1. Read 'disclaimer_and_usage_rules' and the FULL 'change_log' before acting on this project.
2. Treat the newest 'change_log' entries as the current truth. Where they contradict 'project_state', follow the log.
3. Every single time you make ANY change to the project (code, data, structure, config, GitHub, anything), APPEND a new entry to 'change_log' with a medium-sized summary. Never silently skip this.
4. Do NOT rewrite or delete 'project_state' or prune old 'change_log' entries on your own. Only do a full rewrite / cleanup when the user EXPLICITLY tells you to update the whole file.

5. When the user says to update the whole file: rebuild 'project_state' so it fully matches reality, remove/fix outdated info, then CLEAR the 'change_log' back to an empty list and reset 'change_log_next_id' to 1. Bump 'meta.master_version'.
6. Keep entries factual and concise. Use absolute dates (YYYY-MM-DD), not 'today' or 'yesterday'.
7. ALWAYS PUSH TO GITHUB after any change (see 'ALWAYS_PUSH_TO_GITHUB' above). This applies to every change without exception, including changes to this file.

project_state.purpose: Roll30 is a 'D&D Session App' — a shared virtual tabletop where a Game Master prepares and runs Dungeons & Dragons sessions and players interact through a deliberately simplified interface. Its guiding principle is CONTROLLED SIMPLICITY: each person sees only what matters to them right now. The GM builds and controls the world (Build Mode + GM Tools); players get a stripped-down Play Mode. The full product concept is stored verbatim in the repo at CONCEPT.txt.
project_state.current_status: FRONT-END MOCKUP STAGE. Every feature from the concept is built as a professional, accessible UI with realistic but entirely FAKE placeholder data. Nothing is wired to a real backend yet: all reads come from local mock data and all writes (create/save/delete) are intentional no-ops. The app is built so placeholders can be replaced with real functionality with minimal churn. NOTE: it is NOT the old SRD file-explorer and NOT a blank canvas — those were earlier states of this same repo.
project_state.live_url: https://aomarco.github.io/Roll30/ (auto-deploys on push to main; may lag the local mockup until pushed)
project_state.how_the_app_works: React single-page app wrapped by the existing password Gate. Once past the gate, App.tsx routes: with no campaign selected it shows the CampaignsView picker (shared by both roles, with create/join-by-code modals). Opening a campaign splits HARD by role: a Game Master gets CampaignShell (a full sidebar-nav workspace); a player gets PlayerApp (a minimal single-column experience). A GM/Player toggle (RoleSwitch) lets you preview either experience — in a real deployment the role would come from authentication, not a toggle. GM workspace sections: Overview (dashboard), Scenes, Characters, NPCs, Enemies, Items, Shops, Media, Notes & Lore, Templates, Session History. Scenes come in two types: Playing Fields (roleplay: GM-controlled token stage, ambience, prompts, shops) and Battle Fields (tactical: grid map, initiative tracker, context-sensitive action panel, interactive objects + a trigger/effect logic display). Each open scene has a Build/Session mode toggle and a GM Tools drawer for 'play god' overrides (move any token, set HP/conditions, force/cancel effects, edit initiative, undo, rewind, control a PC, reveal/conceal, secret rolls, save-as-template, transition/convert scene). The player experience shows only the current scene, their own character (persistent bottom bar), a compact character sheet, and whatever actions are currently available — including a simplified shop storefront that honours hidden/requirement flags and the shop's automatic/approval/manual checkout mode.

architecture_for_replacing_placeholders.principle: The UI NEVER imports mock data directly. It only ever calls the data layer. Making the app 'real' is therefore localized to two files.
architecture_for_replacing_placeholders.src/types.ts: Complete typed domain model and the contract between UI and data: Campaign, Folder, Scene (PlayingField | BattleField), Token, InitiativeEntry, Interactive, Trigger/TriggerEffect, Shop/ShopItem, Character (full sheet), NPC, Enemy, Item, Media, Note, SessionLog.
architecture_for_replacing_placeholders.src/api.ts: The ONLY data access layer. Promise-based functions (api.campaigns(), api.scene(id), api.characters(), api.shop(id), etc.) plus placeholder mutation stubs api.save()/api.remove(). Replace the bodies with real fetch/Supabase calls and the UI is unchanged.
architecture_for_replacing_placeholders.src/mock/data.ts: The ONLY file that fabricates content (campaign 'The Hollow Crown' + a 4-character party, scenes, enemies, shops, notes, media, session logs). Delete once api.ts talks to a real backend.
architecture_for_replacing_placeholders.src/state.tsx: App-wide UI context: role (gm|player), current campaignId, a small view router, scene mode (build|session), and the seated character for player preview.
architecture_for_replacing_placeholders.placeholder_honesty: Mockup surfaces render a <PlaceholderTag> (dashed 'Placeholder' chip) where it matters, so it is always clear what is not yet wired.

tech_stack.framework: React 18 + TypeScript (strict typecheck enforced in the build)
tech_stack.styling: Tailwind CSS v4 (via @tailwindcss/vite). Custom SERIOUS accessible dark design system in src/index.css: single indigo accent, slate neutral scale, semantic surface tokens, always-visible focus rings, prefers-reduced-motion support. Deliberately NOT the old neon/'radical' theme.
tech_stack.icons: src/ui/icons.tsx — self-contained inline SVG icon set (no icon library; CSP-safe).
tech_stack.animation: Framer Motion is installed but the new UI largely uses CSS transitions.
tech_stack.backend: Supabase (Postgres + PostgREST RPC) — used ONLY for the password gate today. No app/game data is persisted yet.
tech_stack.hosting: GitHub Pages, deployed by GitHub Actions on push to main.

supabase.schema: Table public.authorized_ips (ip text PK, created_at). RLS enabled with NO direct anon/authenticated access. Two SECURITY DEFINER functions: is_ip_authorized(check_ip)->boolean and authorize_ip(pw, client_ip)->boolean (password lives only inside this function).
supabase.password_note: The gate password is 'MarceloIsFranklyAmazing'. It exists ONLY in the authorize_ip DB function, never in the client bundle.
supabase.gate_caveat: Soft gate only: public IPs are shared behind NAT and change over time, so it can auto-admit others on the same network and occasionally re-prompt. Not suitable for truly sensitive data.
supabase.IMPORTANT_do_not_touch: There is a SEPARATE unrelated Supabase project named 'chara' (ref uilqxmsllyugxuzsxwkx) holding chara_* tables. The Roll30 gate lives ONLY in eujhtcnnjtwsthscdfqk. Never modify the chara project for Roll30 work.

structure.CONCEPT.txt: The full product concept for the D&D Session App (source of truth for what to build).
structure.index.html: Vite HTML entry point; title 'Roll30 · D&D Session App'.
structure.package.json: Deps + scripts. build = 'tsc --noEmit && vite build' so type errors cannot slip past esbuild.
structure.vite.config.ts: Vite config; base '/Roll30/' for the GitHub Pages path.
structure.src/main.tsx: App entry; wraps <App/> in the existing <Gate/> (password + IP system still protects everything).
structure.src/App.tsx: Top-level router: campaign picker -> role-split (GM shell vs player app).
structure.src/api.ts: Promise-based data access layer (swap point for a real backend).
structure.src/mock/data.ts: All fake placeholder content (delete when backend is real).
structure.src/state.tsx: App context: role, current campaign, view router, scene mode.
structure.src/index.css: Design system (accessible dark theme + reusable component classes).
structure.src/ui/: icons.tsx (inline SVG set), primitives.tsx (Avatar, Badge, SectionHeading, EmptyState, Skeleton, Field, Modal, Meter, PlaceholderTag), RoleSwitch.tsx.
structure.src/views/: GM views: CampaignsView, CampaignShell, DashboardView, ScenesView, SceneView, PlayingField, BattleField, GMToolsPanel, CharactersView, ResourceView (NPCs/Enemies/Items/Media), ShopsView, NotesView, TemplatesView, HistoryView.
structure.src/gate.ts: Supabase RPC client + public-IP lookup for the password gate.
structure.src/components/Gate.tsx: The simple password lock screen (from the pre-mockup step).
structure..github/workflows/deploy.yml: GitHub Actions: build and deploy to GitHub Pages on push to main.
structure.PROJECT_MASTER.json: This file — master record and change log.
structure.legacy_note: The repo still contains data/ and public/data/ (25 SRD JSON files) and possibly README.md from the old SRD-explorer version. They are not used by the current D&D Session App mockup.

project_state.how_to_run_locally: From C:\Users\Marcelo\Documents\Roll30 run 'npm install' (first time) then 'npm run dev'. 'npm run build' type-checks and builds; 'npm run preview' serves the build. Pushing to main auto-builds and redeploys to GitHub Pages.
project_state.verification_status: As of 2026-07-21: 'tsc --noEmit' passes clean, production build succeeds, and 'vite preview' serves HTTP 200 with the app mounting at #root.

tooling.github_cli: GitHub CLI 2.96.0, authenticated as aomarco (with 'workflow' scope), wired into git via 'gh auth setup-git'
